Question: Letting $$x_{0} = 0 ,$$ let’s use Newton’s method to approximate the root of $$f ( x ) = x^{3} - 3 x + 1$$ over the interval $$[ 0 , 1 ]$$ by calculating $$x_{1}$$ and $$x_{2} .$$
Use Newton’s method to approximate $$\sqrt{3}$$ by letting $$f ( x ) = x^{2} - 3$$ and $$x_{0} = 3 .$$ Find $$x_{1}$$ and $$x_{2} .$$

Consider the function $$F ( x ) = \frac{1}{3} x + 6 .$$ Let $$x_{0} = 0$$ and let $$x_{n} = F ( x_{n - 1} )$$ for $$n \geq 2 .$$ Find $$x_{1} , x_{2} , x_{3} , x_{4} , x_{5} .$$ Make a conjecture about what happens to the list of numbers $$x_{1} , x_{2} , x_{3} \text{,unicode[:ddot.]} x_{n} \text{,unicode[:ddot.]}$$ as $$n \vec{} \infty .$$
